sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("BEFORE white regions:")
for n in before:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nAFTER white regions:")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nAFTER colored markers:")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'N', 'p', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] < 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, r={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, c={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After clicking blue at cols 38-39:
- White at cols 28-35: px 40→24, r=52-57→54-57 (shrunk down)
- White at cols 38-47: px 32→52, r=54-57→52-57 (expanded up)
- Colored markers at cols 41-43: charcoal 51-52→49-50, purple 53→51 (moved UP by 2)
So clicking blue at cols 38-39 moved the white region at cols 38-47 up, which moved the colored markers at cols 41-43 up. This reversed the effect of clicking blue at cols 34-35.
Let me summarize the blue block effects I've observed:
1. Blue at cols 12-13: moves white at cols 6-13 UP (track 1 up)
2. Blue at cols 16-17: moves white at cols 6-13 DOWN (track 1 down)
3. Blue at cols 24-25: merges white regions (cols 18-23 + 28-35 → 16-25 + 30-33)
4. Blue at cols 28-29: reverses effect of blue at cols 24-25
5. Blue at cols 34-35: moves white at cols 38-47 DOWN (track 6 down)
6. Blue at cols 38-39: moves white at cols 38-47 UP (track 6 up)
So the pattern seems to be:
- Blue at cols 12-13 and 16-17: control track 1 (up/down)
- Blue at cols 24-25 and 28-29: control something else (merge/unmerge?)
- Blue at cols 34-35 and 38-39: control track 6 (down/up)
Wait, but the order is inconsistent. For track 1, cols 12-13 is UP and cols 16-17 is DOWN. For track 6, cols 34-35 is DOWN and cols 38-39 is UP.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the blue blocks control the white regions to their LEFT or RIGHT:
- Blue at cols 12-13: right edge of white at cols 6-13 → UP
- Blue at cols 16-17: left edge of white at cols 18-23 → DOWN (affects white at cols 6-13)
- Blue at cols 24-25: right edge of white at cols 18-23 → merge?
- Blue at cols 28-29: left edge of white at cols 28-35 → unmerge?
- Blue at cols 34-35: right edge of white at cols 28-35 → DOWN (affects white at cols 38-47)
- Blue at cols 38-39: left edge of white at cols 38-47 → UP
Hmm, the pattern isn't clear. Let me try clicking the remaining blue blocks to see what they do.
Remaining blue blocks: cols 46-47 and 50-51.
Let me try blue at cols 46-47.
